rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice of Hearing must show the COMPLETE TITLE of the pleading to be heard. (3) Please show the calculation of the amounts to be distributed. If there is $740,126.27 on hand, after setting aside a reserve of $7,500, the distributable amount should be $732,626.27... (4) Distribution to Alice Anivian must be to her estate. Under what authority can distribution be made to the trustee of her trust? (5) The terms of the testamentary trust must be set forth. [CRC 7.650(b).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copy of the petition was received by the assigned examiner, so it had to be reviewed online. There may be additional issues.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEONARDO ROSILLO SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST, CREATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #12, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Investments were purchased without prior court authority. Two of the mutual funds (JOHIX and MIEIX) were described in the petition, and one additional mutual fund was described in the declaration filed on 6/14/24 (DFIEX); however, as noted in the prior tentative ruling, many more investments were made (e.g. ISHARES TR S&P NTL AMT FREE, ISHARES TR CORE MSCI EAFE, ISHARES TR RUSELL 1000, etc), which were not any of the additional investments allowed under the 9/12/22 order approving the 11th account. 2) Additional information regarding the $14,800 reimbursed to the beneficiary's mother for HVAC installation was provided. Court to determine reasonableness. 3) $9,805 was reimbursed to the beneficiary's mother for vacations. Only $4,500 of the amount was described in the declaration filed on 6/14/24, and the amount described was for the benefit of 3 other family members. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues remain unaddressed: 1) Paragraph 15 of the instant petition states that an election pursuant to Probate Code Section 13502 will be filed by the surviving spouse prior to the hearing, but no such election has been filed. 2) Paragraph 19 on the instant petition states that waivers of accounting by the heirs and Rebekah Mark will be filed prior to the hearing, but no such waivers have been filed. 3) Paragraph 23 alludes to a declaration supporting an extraordinary fee request by petitioner's counsel, but no such declaration has been filed. A petition for extraordinary compensation must include, or be accompanied by, a statement of facts upon which the petition is based (CRC 7.702). 4) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. (LRSF 14.35.I.6.a). 5) A petition for distribution must list and describe in detail the property to be distributed, in the body of the petition or in an attachment that is incorporated by reference (CRC 7.651(a)). 6) No proposed order has been received (LRSF 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

The names and dates of death of the predeceased spouse's parents, siblings, and their issue who would have been heirs of the predeceased spouse if they were alive must be included in the family tree.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTORIA C. DRAYTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Limited Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Cassonja Wiley, a nominee of the decedent's niece (who is one of the heirs), seeks her appointment as successor administrator with limited IAEA authority. Cassonja Wiley's petition is silent, however, as to her bond. A full bond, if required, must be fixed at $671,000 (rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars), whether limited or full IAEA authority, as the estate real property has already been sold. If Cassonja Wiley is unable or unwilling to give a full bond, the Court is to determine whether to deny IAEA authority and whether to require prior court approval for taking possession of money or other property of the estate, as set forth at item 5d of the proposed order (DE-140 form), in light of LRSF 14.16(M)(3) which provides that: "Because of the difficulties of monitoring the issuance of Letters based on orders requiring blocked accounts, the Court discourages the use of blocked accounts on orders for probate and for appointment of a conservator." Prior court approval for taking possession of money can be sought by drop-off ex parte petition to deposit funds in insured blocked accounts after letters are issued. Other procedural issues that remain unaddressed include: 1) The address of Tyron Nunley (heir whose share is 1/2800) listed on the proof of service (DE-121 form) is different from his address the Court has on file (correctional facility in St. Louis, MI). Attachment 8 to the petition listed his address as: c/o Janelle Nunley, 7000 Parkerest Drive #302, Westland, MI. See CRC 7.51(a)(2) ("A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person."). 2) The address of Terrence Capers listed on the proof of service (DE-121 form) is different from his address listed on Attachment 8 to the petition (416 Downey Ave. Apt. 5A, Modesto, CA). 3) Sheila Foster, the person having legal custody of and residing with Ean Foster, a minor (heir whose share is 1/840), was not listed on the proof of service (DE-121 form). See CRC 7.51(d) ("notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides."). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NENA M. LIOTTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. NB: The attorney and the executor would have been entitled to $7,049.25 statutory compensation based on the I&A alone. When statutory compensation is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale are required to be filed. [CRC 7.705(b).] The amendment and proof of service refer to the beneficiary as the "sole heir." The sole heir is the executor; the granddaughter is the sole beneficiary.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEVIN Y. HOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (3) No proof of publication has been filed. (4) The Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities must be filed. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) The petition caption is incomplete if IAEA authority is being requested. (7) Items 3a, b, and c of the petition were not answered. (If the decedent resided in Pacifica, this is not the proper venue.) (8) Item 3h of the petition was not completed. (9) Item 6 of the petition must be completed, if no spouse or issue survived. (10) Item 8 must include all persons mentioned in the will, including an alternate executor. (11) The alternate executor must be given notice. (12) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who is/was the petitioner's parent who was the decedent's sibling? Did the decedent have other siblings? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/12/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES H. SPALDING JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioners state that Probate Code 6124 "does not apply" because, among other things, Decedent's desk was messy, and he may have inadvertently thrown it away. Court to determine whether 6124 applies and, if it does, whether Petitioners have offered evidence to rebut the presumption. 2) The will is dated 7/13/15 but the signature is dated 8/27/15. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
